title: The Sleepy-Time Science Experiment
summary: Bunsen Honeydew and Beaker are determined to stay awake to conduct scientific experiments, but they eventually decide to turn their sleepiness into a study of lullabies. After testing several songs, Beaker falls fast asleep. Bunsen then listens to a soothing 'sleepcast' about the peaceful activities at a local laundromat on Sesame Street, where friends fold laundry and wait patiently for their blankets to be clean. Through the calming sounds of folding and rhythmic breathing, the restless scientists find the relaxation they need.

transcript.text: Let's get it all set up here. I'm going to bring it over a little closer.

transcript.text: Dad, let's practice turning off the light first and then turning on the phone and then putting on the lantern.
Turn off the... well but I won't be able to see if the light's out. That's why I put this on first, and then I turn off the light.

Izzy
transcript.text: Most we can... well I can use my flashlight.

transcript.text: Oh, that's true, that would be a good solution to that problem, you're right. Okay.
Why are you shaking it?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, I was just moving it so I could sit by you, because I thought I'd want to be a little closer to you over here.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Hey, hey, let's tell a story about Beaker and Bunsen having trouble falling asleep.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Because they want to do science.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Izzy
transcript.text: I'll start it for you. Once upon a time, Bunsen and Beaker were falling asleep, but they had one problem. It was that they couldn't go to sleep because they wanted to do more science. Now it's your turn, Dad.

Oh, okay. And Bunsen said to Beaker, 'I can't sleep, I just want to do science.' And Beaker said, 'Meep, meep, meep, meep.' And Bunsen said, 'Oh, that's a very interesting idea, yeah, we could try that.'
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Let's try a lullaby. Beaker said a lullaby.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Bunsen said, 'Yes, we could even do a little bit of sleepy time science by testing out different lullabies to see which one puts us to sleep.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's test out Rock-a-bye on the Treetops one day. Rock-a-bye Bunsen and Beaker on the treetops, one night the stars were bright and the moon was light, Taroy. That's how that lullaby goes.

And Bunsen said, 'Hmm, well, I enjoyed the lullaby and it did make me feel a little bit sleepy, but I am still awake. Are you awake, Beaker?' 'Meep, meep.' 'Now, I think that means we should try another lullaby to see if it puts us to sleep.'
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Oh, Dad, remember the one that you or mom used to sing when I was a little kid? We can try singing that to Bunsen and Beaker.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Okay. Little Beaker, meep-er, it's time to sleep. Little Beaker, meep-er, little Beaker, meep-er. Meep, meep, meep, meep, meep, meep, meep, meep, meep, meep, meep, meep. Little Beaker, meep-er, it's time to sleep. It's time. How'd we do?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Great. Did anyone go to sleep? 'Zzz,' says Beaker.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my goodness! What a wonderful result of our experiment, said Bunsen. You're sleeping, Beaker! Congratulations!
Now you've just got to make you fall asleep, Bunsen.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh yes, how are we going to do that?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Let's... um, I think I can put on a sleep story for you, Bunsen.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, thank you.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: This is some sleep stories.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I'm very curious to listen to a sleep story. I've never heard one before.
Bunsen, is this making you feel sleepy yet?

Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a little bit. I'm enjoying it, says Bunsen.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I think once you hear this sleep part on then you might feel a bit more sleepier than usual.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bunsen, is this making you sleepy yet?
Not yet, I'm just listening to the story and enjoying it, but I'm not falling asleep. Is it making you sleepy?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: No. Let's... but... but I'm expecting a while during that Ernie. Yeah, the sleep... then we can also try the sleep book.

transcript.text: Oh, the Dr. Seuss book?

transcript.text: Yeah.

Dad

After stars and stories.

transcript.text: Okay.

I'm going to finish the Sesame Street story.

Dad

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It says 'put on your flashlight'.

It's a cat bisk.

transcript.text: There must have been a cat in your bed.

Izzy
transcript.text: Finn was in my bed for a short nap.

transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Is this making you sleepy yet?
It is! Is it making you sleepy?

Izzy
transcript.text: Daddy, do you know where Baby Bunny is?
Is she in my bed?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: No, she should be in my—
Oh, is that Baby Bunny?

characters.name: Bunsen
characters.description: An obsessive chronometeorologist who views sleep as a luxury the world cannot afford, they spend their days and sunless nights tethered to a brass-bound "time wind tracker." With eyes bloodshot from perpetual vigilance, they monitor the shifting currents of temporal drafts, sacrificing rest to calibrate their instruments and fortify their laboratory against the encroaching gale of an impending storm.

characters.name: Beaker
characters.description: A high-strung Muppet and devoted companion to Bunsen, this jittery assistant—and close friend to Kitty—communicates solely through a frantic cadence of staccato "meeps" and "me-me" sounds. Driven by a nervous obsession with their work, they share a home with the scientist, remaining perpetually wide-eyed and sleepless until the day’s experiments reach a successful conclusion, pausing only for the occasional, coveted treat from the local bakery.

characters.name: Alan
characters.description: A calm friend from Sesame Street who narrates sleep stories.

characters.name: Bert
characters.description: Stepping from the quiet ink of a library book to join the trek into the deep dark, this classic figure from a shared basement apartment maintains the rhythmic precision and meditative focus of a sleep-story weaver. Formerly the host of grand pigeon galas who folded his roommate Ernie’s vibrant chaos into serene harmony, he now applies a caretaker’s grace to the encroaching shadows. Whether smoothing mud-stained hems into crisp geometries or sorting through the abyss’s chaotic clutter, he treats the exploration of the unknown as a soothing ritual—a singular, exacting discipline of folding the world’s wilder reaches back into a state of restorative order.

characters.name: Ernie
characters.description: Bert’s classic companion and roommate moves through their shared world with the jittery stealth of a mouse, their impatient haste betraying them with a trail of unintended squeaks as they assist in the search for the lost pearl. Often found in a futile pursuit of order—whether frantically sweeping thick mud or overwhelmed by a cascade of forgotten toys—this iconic figure remains a soul perpetually caught between domestic chaos and a sudden drenching from a garden hose. Yet, on the long journey for the treasure, their nervous energy settles into a rhythmic wonder; they trade the mess of the earth for the fixed, silent patterns of the cosmos, weaving evocative stories of the stars to guide their path.

characters.name: Cookie Monster
characters.description: This azure beast of Sesame Street, driven by an eternal craving for cookies and perpetually swaddled in a plush blanket peppered with illustrations of his favorite treats, inadvertently snuffed out the constellations with a clumsy flick of a switch during a moonlit drive to a Lego assembly.

characters.name: The Count
characters.description: A character mentioned as having a blanket decorated with numbers for counting.

characters.name: Baby Bunny
characters.description: A toy or character searched for at the end of the evening.

places.name: Laundromat
places.description: A soapy-smelling place where characters go to wash, dry, and fold their colorful laundry.

places.name: Treetops
places.description: The setting for a traditional lullaby where the stars are bright and the moon is light.

places.name: Sesame Street
places.description: A welcoming neighborhood of weathered brownstones where the hum of the corner laundromat follows neighbors on their evening walk home. This friendly urban sanctuary serves as the lively backdrop for the lives of Bert and Ernie, blending the cozy domesticity of their shared apartment with the vibrant, rhythmic pulse of a close-knit community.
